summary: This is an extremely brief blog post that serves as a teaser for Joel Spolsky's February column in Inc. Magazine. The post contains virtually no content beyond a single quote about bureaucratic inertia, referencing Newton's first law of motion as a metaphor. The full argument and context exist in the linked Inc. Magazine column rather than in the blog post itself.
key_points: Bureaucratic organizations tend to resist or ignore the momentum of existing systems and processes
Newton's first law serves as a metaphor for organizational inertia — things already moving should be left to continue
The post is essentially a promotional pointer to Spolsky's Inc. Magazine column
money_quotes: The bureaucrats in Washington had forgotten Newton's first law: An object in motion tends to remain in motion.
key_insight: Bureaucratic interference often disrupts systems that would function better if left to their existing momentum.
